title: [ariados] Battle Guide
summary: A [bug]/[poison] support lead that doubles down on [sticky-web] and [toxic-spikes] to cripple the opposing team's speed and HP for the whole match.
strengths: [sticky-web] plus [toxic-spikes] both available from a single slot
[insomnia] shuts down sleep-powder and spore leads
weaknesses: Low Speed and thin bulk make lead duels risky
Stacked Fire, Flying, Rock, and Psychic weaknesses
Only 90 Attack — damage output is minimal beyond setup

recommendedMoves.reason: Lowers the Speed of every grounded opposing switch-in
Inflicts [poison] on every opposing entry for two layers
[poison] STAB for Fairy and Grass walls

recommendedItems.reason: Guarantees at least one hazard lands from the slot
Absorbs one taunt to preserve the hazard plan

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[ariados] is a [bug]/[poison] support lead whose entire purpose is to stack two hazards. Its stats are modest, but the combination of these two hazards is unusual: most [sticky-web] setters can't also drop [toxic-spikes], and most [toxic-spikes] users lack [sticky-web]. That uniqueness is what earns [ariados] a lead role in dedicated hazard-focused team archetypes, even in the Champions meta.

## Strengths
[sticky-web] lowers the Speed of every grounded switch-in by one stage, turning the opposing team's Speed tier down a notch and giving slower offensive partners on your team the advantage in every Speed race. Combined with two layers of [toxic-spikes], the opposing team is under constant pressure from both angles. [insomnia] is also a strong lead-specific ability, shutting down the common sleep-powder and spore leads that would normally disable [ariados] entirely.

## Weaknesses & Answers
[ariados] is extremely fragile. 70/70/70 defenses with just 40 Speed means any lead that goes first can remove [ariados] before its setup completes. The weakness list. 90 Attack is too low to pressure most walls meaningfully, so the only real offensive utility is [sucker-punch] priority on weakened opponents.

## Recommended Moveset
[sticky-web] is the locked utility slot as it's the primary reason to run [ariados]. [toxic-spikes] is mandatory to stack hazards, and running two layers maximizes the pressure across an entire match. [sucker-punch] fills the offense slot as a priority revenge tool, since [ariados]'s low Speed means any direct attack move will usually go second. [poison-jab] is the coverage slot for Fairy and Grass walls that otherwise shrug off [ariados]'s utility entirely.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[focus-sash] is the default lead item. [mental-herb] is the alternate pick that absorbs a single taunt attempt, which is the main thing that would shut [ariados] out of the match. The ability must be [insomnia] for the sleep immunity, since the other options don't offer meaningful utility. Tera [dark] flips the Psychic weakness, while Tera [steel] erases multiple weaknesses at once.

## Teammates & Synergy
[ariados]'s whole purpose is enabling a specific team archetype. Low-Speed tanks like Snorlax or Tauros gain significantly from the hazard setup. A rapid-spin or defog user should NOT be on the [ariados] team, since that would undo the hazards. Instead, pair [ariados] with strong breakers who punish the poisoned opposing switches.

## Play Patterns
Lead [ariados], drop [sticky-web] turn one, then [toxic-spikes] turn two. If the opposing lead threatens taunt, use [mental-herb] pre-emptively. After the two setup turns, use [sucker-punch] as a revenge option on weakened attackers, or pivot [ariados] out. Save the Tera for the exact moment a Fire or Flying move is about to land, and use it defensively to extend [ariados]'s lead turn.
